title: SQL Analysis & Reporting
subtitle: Turn single-table data into calculated, grouped, and decision-ready business reports.
intendedFor: Learners who completed SQL Foundations / SQL V2 and are ready to build practical business reports without joins.

courseOverview.recommendedSequence: Complete modules in order: readable row-level reports, null-safe calculations, aggregate KPIs, grouped scorecards, HAVING and conditional reporting, then the final capstone.
courseOverview.summary: Learners progressively transform one realistic sales table into trustworthy detail reports, KPI snapshots, grouped scorecards, exception reports, and a final executive report.

moduleSummary.title: Readable Report Foundations
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Bridge SQL Foundations into reporting by teaching learners to shape readable result columns before introducing aggregation.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build an Order Value Detail Report in progressive steps, ending with readable headings and rounded gross sales.

moduleSummary.title: Null-Safe Calculations and Business Labels
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners that report calculations must define how missing values behave before those calculations are summarized.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a Discount Audit Report that exposes missing values, calculates net sales, and assigns review labels.

moduleSummary.title: Aggregate Functions and KPI Snapshots
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Move learners from row-level calculations to one-row KPI summaries while preserving clear report grain.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a Storewide KPI Snapshot that returns one trusted row of headline business metrics.

moduleSummary.title: Grouped Summaries and Ranked Scorecards
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach report grain explicitly so learners can build grouped summaries without accidental duplication or over-grouping.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a Region and Category Scorecard with grouped KPIs and a stable performance ranking.

moduleSummary.title: HAVING and Conditional Reporting
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Complete the single-table reporting toolkit by separating row filters from group filters and adding conditional aggregation.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a Performance Exception Report that surfaces only groups requiring management attention.

moduleSummary.title: Final Capstone: Executive Sales Performance Report
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Integrate readable output, null-safe calculations, aggregate functions, grouping, HAVING, CASE, and conditional aggregation in one portfolio-quality report.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Complete the Executive Sales Performance Report as a progressive final capstone with a validated final query and written interpretation.

authoringGuidance: This course must always use subjectSlug sql, courseSlug sql-analysis-reporting, catalogSlug sql, and profileId sql.
The generated draft subject slug is sql--sql-analysis-reporting--draft; do not confuse it with the authoring subjectSlug/courseSlug split.
Use browser-sql-runner and sql-browser-workspace across every module. Learners write in the SQL editor, click Run query, and inspect the results table.
Every technical sketch must have a required embedded Try It code_input with tryItPlacement all_sketches unless the topic is explicitly conceptual-only.
Embedded Try It IDs, tryIt.id, and tryIt.exerciseKey must use try-<topic-id>-sketchN.
Quizzes are conceptual and must not use code_input; graded SQL belongs in Try It, practice, module projects, or capstone steps.
Every standard module has exactly one module project. The final module is a dedicated capstone and must not be generated as an ordinary module project.
Module projects and capstones are progressive: step N+1 starterCode must equal step N full solutionCode. Solutions are full snapshots, never diffs.
Every SQL checker must validate expected column names, row values, row count, and deterministic order when ordering is part of the requirement.
Use lesson examples, Try It tasks, module projects, and the capstone for different reporting questions. Do not simply change names while preserving the exact same query shape and thresholds.
Keep the entire course single-table on sales_kpi.sales_reporting. JOIN, subquery, CTE, window functions, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and CREATE TABLE are reserved for later courses.
Do not use terminal, command-line, sqlite3, psql, SQL file, or run-file instructions.
State report grain in beginner-friendly language whenever GROUP BY is introduced: what one row in the results table represents.
Do not hide NULL behavior. Every fallback must be justified by the reporting requirement.
Use exact aliases consistently across prompt, starter query comments, solution query, expected columns, and feedback.

assessmentAndDelivery.suggestedBeginnerRhythm: Two technical topics per week followed by the cumulative module project; reserve a longer final block for the capstone.
recommendedCourseDeliverables: Embedded Try It query on every technical sketch
Final executive sales performance capstone with written findings

moduleMilestones.learnersCan: Alias, calculate, round, and order row-level report columns.
Use COALESCE and CASE to create trustworthy row-level values and labels.
Summarize a filtered dataset with aggregate functions.
Control report grain with GROUP BY and rank groups deterministically.
Use HAVING and conditional aggregation to surface actionable groups.
Plan, build, validate, and explain a complete executive SQL report.

assessmentAndDelivery.closingNote: Learners finish ready for the next course, Multi-Table SQL, where the same reporting skills are applied across related tables.

notes: Keep every query at row-level detail.
Calculated columns must have stable aliases and deterministic expected values.
Teach the business meaning of NULL before choosing a fallback.
CASE remains row-level in this module.
All aggregate practice returns one summary row.
Explicitly compare COUNT(*) with COUNT(nullable_column).
Every grouped query must state what one result row represents.
Use deterministic secondary sorting.
Require clear separation between row filters and group filters.
Conditional KPIs must preserve the intended group grain.
The final capstone must integrate the full single-table reporting toolkit.
Every capstone step must chain from the previous complete solution query.

description: A cumulative single-table SQL reporting course covering aliases, calculations, NULL safety, aggregate functions, GROUP BY, HAVING, CASE, conditional aggregation, module projects, and a final executive-report capstone.
prerequisites: Read a single database table and identify rows and columns.
Select specific columns and use readable filters.
Combine conditions with AND, OR, and NOT.
Sort and limit results and work with LIKE, IN, BETWEEN, and IS NULL.
learningGoals: Create readable result columns with aliases, calculations, and rounding.
Handle missing values deliberately with COALESCE.
Build one-row KPI snapshots with aggregate functions.
Build grouped scorecards with GROUP BY and deterministic sorting.
Filter grouped reports with HAVING and calculate conditional KPIs with CASE.
Complete five cumulative module projects and one final executive-report capstone.

notes: Every technical sketch must receive a required embedded Try It code_input with id/exerciseKey formatted as try-<topic-id>-sketchN.
Conceptual-only topics may omit Try It, but quizzes remain non-codeInput.
Every standard module must contain exactly one role=module_project section with one progressive project topic.
The final module must contain exactly one role=capstone section and no ordinary module project section.
Module projects and capstone steps are progressive: step N+1 starterCode must equal step N full solutionCode.
Lesson examples, Try It prompts, module projects, and the capstone must use different reporting questions, grouping targets, conditions, or thresholds.
All graded SQL must produce deterministic expected columns, rows, values, and order.
Use only the sales_kpi dataset and its sales_reporting table. Do not introduce joins, subqueries, CTEs, window functions, or data mutation.

requiredDataCharacteristics: At least four regions and four product categories.
At least three order_status values including Completed and Pending.
Repeated region-category combinations for meaningful grouping.
Some NULL discount_pct values and some NULL customer_rating values.
Ties in at least one grouped KPI so secondary sorting can be tested.
Enough groups above and below HAVING thresholds used by projects.

globalRules: Every technical sketch must have a required embedded Try It unless explicitly conceptual-only.
Try It IDs and exercise keys must start with try- and follow try-<topic-id>-sketchN.
Every standard module must contain exactly one role=module_project section.
The final module must contain exactly one role=capstone section.
Module project and capstone step N+1 starterCode must equal step N solutionCode.
Every expected result must be generated from the seeded sales_kpi dataset and query only the sales_reporting table.
All reports must be single-table and must not use disallowed concepts.
When output order matters, expected rows must be order-stable and the query must include deterministic tie-breaking.

notes: Keep every query at row-level detail.
Calculated columns must have stable aliases and deterministic expected values.
Teach the business meaning of NULL before choosing a fallback.
CASE remains row-level in this module.
All aggregate practice returns one summary row.
Explicitly compare COUNT(*) with COUNT(nullable_column).
Every grouped query must state what one result row represents.
Use deterministic secondary sorting.
Require clear separation between row filters and group filters.
Conditional KPIs must preserve the intended group grain.
The final capstone must integrate the full single-table reporting toolkit.
Every capstone step must chain from the previous complete solution query.

modules.title: Readable Report Foundations
modules.description: Turn raw columns into clear, calculated report output with aliases, DISTINCT, arithmetic expressions, and rounding.
modules.purpose: Bridge SQL Foundations into reporting by teaching learners to shape readable result columns before introducing aggregation.
learningObjectives: Rename result columns with clear aliases.
Use DISTINCT to discover report categories without duplicate values.
Create calculated columns from existing numeric fields.
Round calculated values for readable business reports.
guidedExercises: Rename order fields for a manager-facing detail report.
List unique regions, categories, statuses, and sales representatives.
Calculate gross line value from quantity and unit price.
Round monetary expressions without changing the source data.
modules.moduleProject: Build an Order Value Detail Report in progressive steps, ending with readable headings and rounded gross sales.

sections.title: Readable Report Output
sections.description: Make raw result sets easier for another person to read and reuse.

topics.title: Aliases for Readable Headings
topics.summary: Use AS to replace database-oriented column names with clear report headings.
learningGoals: Write column aliases with and without the optional AS keyword.
Choose stable, descriptive aliases that match the report requirement.
Explain that aliases change result headings, not stored column names.

topics.title: Distinct Values for Report Categories
topics.summary: Use DISTINCT to inspect the unique categories available for a report.
learningGoals: Return unique values from one column.
Use DISTINCT across more than one selected column and explain the resulting combinations.
Avoid using DISTINCT as a substitute for understanding duplicate rows.

sections.title: Calculated Report Columns
sections.description: Create useful numbers directly in a SELECT result.

topics.title: Arithmetic Expressions in SELECT
topics.summary: Calculate gross sales and other row-level metrics from numeric columns.
learningGoals: Use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division inside SELECT.
Use parentheses to make calculation order explicit.
Give every calculated expression a readable alias.

topics.title: Rounding Report Values
topics.summary: Use ROUND to present calculated numbers at a practical level of precision.
learningGoals: Round a numeric expression to a fixed number of decimal places.
Keep the unrounded source values unchanged.
Explain why display precision matters in financial and operational reports.

sections.title: Module Project
sections.description: Build a manager-ready order detail report one query improvement at a time.

topics.title: Module 0: Order Value Detail Report
topics.summary: Create a clean order detail report with selected columns, readable aliases, gross sales, and stable sorting.
learningGoals: Select only the fields required by the reporting brief.
Calculate and round gross sales for every order row.
Produce a final deterministic report ordered for review.

projectBrief.scenario: A sales manager needs a clean detail report before the weekly meeting, but the raw table headings and values are not presentation-ready.
projectBrief.workspace: Browser SQL editor, schema browser, and deterministic results table
projectBrief.deliverable: A single-table order detail query with exact headings, calculated gross sales, and deterministic row order.
requirements: Use only concepts taught in Module 0 and SQL Foundations.
Use exactly five progressive steps that match the project stepCountTarget.
Keep every order row in the report; do not add WHERE filters, DISTINCT, or LIMIT to this project.
Use a different reporting question from the worked lesson examples and Try It exercises.
Carry each full working query into the next project step.
Validate exact columns, aliases, values, and row order.

modules.title: Null-Safe Calculations and Business Labels
modules.description: Build trustworthy calculations when values are missing, then turn numeric results into readable business labels.
modules.purpose: Teach learners that report calculations must define how missing values behave before those calculations are summarized.
learningObjectives: Predict how NULL affects arithmetic expressions.
Use COALESCE to provide explicit fallback values.
Calculate percentages and discounts safely.
Use CASE to produce readable categories and status labels.
guidedExercises: Compare calculations with and without missing discount values.
Replace a missing discount percentage with zero using COALESCE.
Calculate discount amount and net sales.
Label rows with CASE based on status or calculated value.
modules.moduleProject: Build a Discount Audit Report that exposes missing values, calculates net sales, and assigns review labels.

sections.title: Null-Safe Calculations
sections.description: Make the treatment of missing values explicit and testable.

topics.title: How NULL Affects Calculations
topics.summary: Understand why arithmetic with an unknown value produces an unknown result.
learningGoals: Predict the result of arithmetic expressions containing NULL.
Distinguish a missing value from a numeric zero.
Identify report columns that need an explicit fallback rule.

topics.title: COALESCE for Missing Values
topics.summary: Use COALESCE to choose the first available value and keep calculations usable.
learningGoals: Use COALESCE with a nullable numeric column.
Choose a fallback value that matches the business meaning.
Explain when replacing NULL with zero would be misleading.

sections.title: Business Calculations and Labels
sections.description: Turn row-level data into net values and readable classifications.

topics.title: Percentage and Discount Calculations
topics.summary: Calculate discount amount and net sales with clear parentheses and aliases.
learningGoals: Convert a percentage value into a decimal calculation.
Calculate gross sales, discount amount, and net sales.
Round final monetary output while preserving calculation logic.

topics.title: CASE for Readable Labels
topics.summary: Translate conditions into report-friendly categories such as Review, Standard, and Priority.
learningGoals: Write a searched CASE expression with multiple WHEN branches.
Provide an ELSE branch for unmatched rows.
Use a calculated or stored value to assign a readable label.

sections.title: Module Project
sections.description: Create a review queue for orders with missing or unusual discount data.

topics.title: Module 1: Discount Audit Report
topics.summary: Calculate gross sales, discount amount, and net sales while clearly labeling rows that need review.
learningGoals: Use COALESCE according to an explicit business rule.
Calculate and round gross, discount, and net values.
Assign a CASE-based audit label and sort the review queue predictably.

projectBrief.scenario: The finance team suspects that missing or unusually large discount values are making the weekly sales report unreliable.
projectBrief.workspace: Browser SQL editor, schema browser, and deterministic results table
projectBrief.deliverable: A deterministic discount audit query with null-safe calculations and a clear review label.
requirements: Use only SQL Foundations plus Module 0 and Module 1 concepts.
Do not copy the same conditions or thresholds used in lesson examples.
Carry each project step forward as a complete query.
Validate exact numeric results, aliases, labels, and row order.

modules.title: Aggregate Functions and KPI Snapshots
modules.description: Summarize an entire filtered dataset with counts, totals, averages, minimums, and maximums.
modules.purpose: Move learners from row-level calculations to one-row KPI summaries while preserving clear report grain.
learningObjectives: Use COUNT(*) and COUNT(column) intentionally.
Calculate totals with SUM.
Calculate averages and explain NULL behavior.
Find smallest and largest values with MIN and MAX.
guidedExercises: Count all rows and compare that result with a nullable-column count.
Sum quantities and net sales expressions.
Calculate average order value for a filtered set.
Return minimum and maximum prices, ratings, or calculated values.
modules.moduleProject: Build a Storewide KPI Snapshot that returns one trusted row of headline business metrics.

sections.title: Counts and Totals
sections.description: Measure how much activity exists and how much value it represents.

topics.title: Counting Rows and Values
topics.summary: Choose between COUNT(*) and COUNT(column) based on the question being answered.
learningGoals: Count every row in a filtered result.
Count only non-NULL values in a specific column.
Explain why the two counts can differ.

topics.title: Summing Business Metrics
topics.summary: Use SUM to total stored values and calculated expressions.
learningGoals: Sum a numeric column such as quantity.
Sum a row-level sales expression.
Apply WHERE before SUM to summarize the intended population.

sections.title: Averages and Extremes
sections.description: Describe typical and boundary values in a dataset.

topics.title: Averages and NULL Behavior
topics.summary: Use AVG and explain which rows contribute to the result.
learningGoals: Calculate an average from a numeric column or expression.
Explain that AVG ignores NULL inputs.
Avoid replacing NULL values unless the business meaning requires it.

topics.title: Minimums and Maximums
topics.summary: Use MIN and MAX to identify the range of observed values.
learningGoals: Return minimum and maximum values from a filtered result.
Apply MIN and MAX to calculated expressions.
Interpret the result in plain business language.

sections.title: Module Project
sections.description: Combine multiple aggregate expressions into one executive snapshot.

topics.title: Module 2: Storewide KPI Snapshot
topics.summary: Produce one row containing order count, units sold, gross sales, net sales, average order value, and value range.
learningGoals: Choose an aggregate that matches each KPI definition.
Use null-safe row expressions inside aggregates where required.
Return one deterministic KPI row with exact aliases and rounded values.

projectBrief.scenario: A store director needs a compact headline snapshot before reviewing any regional breakdowns.
projectBrief.workspace: Browser SQL editor, schema browser, and deterministic results table
projectBrief.deliverable: A one-row KPI query with correctly defined counts, totals, averages, minimums, and maximums.
requirements: Use only concepts taught through Module 2.
Keep the report at one-row grain; do not introduce GROUP BY yet.
Build the query progressively and preserve every prior working metric.
Validate exact values and aliases against the seeded dataset.

modules.title: Grouped Summaries and Ranked Scorecards
modules.description: Use GROUP BY to calculate KPIs for each region, category, status, or carefully chosen combination.
modules.purpose: Teach report grain explicitly so learners can build grouped summaries without accidental duplication or over-grouping.
learningObjectives: Group aggregate results by one column.
Group by more than one column and describe the resulting grain.
Sort grouped summaries by an aggregate result.
Recognize and fix common grouping mistakes.
guidedExercises: Count orders by region.
Calculate net sales by region and category.
Rank groups by total or average value.
Compare correct grouping with an overly detailed result.
modules.moduleProject: Build a Region and Category Scorecard with grouped KPIs and a stable performance ranking.

sections.title: Grouping Basics
sections.description: Turn one overall summary into one summary row per meaningful group.

topics.title: GROUP BY One Column
topics.summary: Create one aggregate result row for each distinct value in a chosen category.
learningGoals: Group counts, sums, and averages by one column.
Explain the grain of the grouped result.
Use clear aliases for every aggregate expression.

topics.title: GROUP BY Multiple Columns
topics.summary: Create one summary row for each meaningful combination of values.
learningGoals: Group by two columns in a deliberate order.
Describe how adding a grouping column changes result grain.
Choose group columns that match the reporting question.

sections.title: Ranking and Grouping Quality
sections.description: Order grouped reports and prevent accidental over-grouping.

topics.title: Sorting Aggregate Results
topics.summary: Rank grouped rows by totals, averages, or counts while keeping ties deterministic.
learningGoals: Order a grouped result by an aggregate alias.
Use a secondary sort to make tied values deterministic.
Choose ascending or descending order based on the business question.

topics.title: Avoiding Grouping Mistakes
topics.summary: Recognize selected columns that do not belong at the intended report grain.
learningGoals: Identify a selected column that makes a grouped report too detailed.
Explain why every non-aggregated selected column must match the group grain.
Refactor a query to produce the intended summary rows.

sections.title: Module Project
sections.description: Create a ranked scorecard for regional and category performance.

topics.title: Module 3: Region and Category Scorecard
topics.summary: Group sales activity by region and category, calculate several KPIs, and rank the final scorecard.
learningGoals: Define and preserve region-category report grain.
Calculate order count, units, net sales, and average order value per group.
Use deterministic ranking with a secondary sort.

projectBrief.scenario: Regional leaders need a scorecard that compares product categories without exposing individual order rows.
projectBrief.workspace: Browser SQL editor, schema browser, and deterministic results table
projectBrief.deliverable: A grouped region-category scorecard with exact KPIs, aliases, and stable ranking.
requirements: Use one source table only.
Use only concepts taught through Module 3.
Do not reuse the same grouping combination used in the worked lesson or Try It exercise.
Carry every completed metric into the next project step.

modules.title: HAVING and Conditional Reporting
modules.description: Filter grouped results, calculate conditional KPIs, and turn aggregate results into performance bands.
modules.purpose: Complete the single-table reporting toolkit by separating row filters from group filters and adding conditional aggregation.
learningObjectives: Use WHERE to filter source rows before grouping.
Use HAVING to filter groups after aggregation.
Build conditional counts and totals with CASE inside aggregate functions.
Assign performance labels from aggregate results.
guidedExercises: Compare a row-level WHERE condition with an aggregate HAVING condition.
Keep only groups that meet minimum activity or value thresholds.
Count completed and pending orders conditionally.
Label grouped performance with CASE based on aggregate results.
modules.moduleProject: Build a Performance Exception Report that surfaces only groups requiring management attention.

sections.title: Filtering Before and After Grouping
sections.description: Place each condition at the stage where it logically belongs.

topics.title: WHERE Before Grouping
topics.summary: Filter source rows before aggregate functions calculate grouped results.
learningGoals: Explain the order in which WHERE and GROUP BY affect a query.
Use WHERE to define the population included in every group.
Predict how changing the row filter changes aggregate values.

topics.title: HAVING After Grouping
topics.summary: Filter grouped rows using aggregate conditions such as minimum count or sales total.
learningGoals: Write a HAVING condition using COUNT, SUM, or AVG.
Explain why an aggregate condition does not belong in WHERE.
Combine WHERE and HAVING when both source rows and final groups need filtering.

sections.title: Conditional KPIs and Labels
sections.description: Calculate multiple business outcomes from one grouped result.

topics.title: CASE Inside Aggregate Functions
topics.summary: Use conditional aggregation to count or total only rows matching a condition.
learningGoals: Build a conditional count with SUM(CASE WHEN ... THEN 1 ELSE 0 END).
Build a conditional total with SUM(CASE WHEN ... THEN expression ELSE 0 END).
Keep each conditional KPI aligned with the grouped report grain.

topics.title: Performance Bands from Aggregates
topics.summary: Use CASE with aggregate results to assign readable group-level performance labels.
learningGoals: Assign labels from grouped totals or averages.
Order CASE conditions from most specific to broadest.
Use an ELSE branch so every group receives a label.

sections.title: Module Project
sections.description: Surface low-volume, low-value, or low-completion groups for management review.

topics.title: Module 4: Performance Exception Report
topics.summary: Build a grouped exception report with row filtering, HAVING thresholds, conditional KPIs, and performance labels.
learningGoals: Use WHERE and HAVING for different filtering stages.
Calculate completed-order count and completion rate conditionally.
Return only management-relevant groups in deterministic priority order.

projectBrief.scenario: Operations leaders do not want another full scorecard; they need a focused list of groups that require action.
projectBrief.workspace: Browser SQL editor, schema browser, and deterministic results table
projectBrief.deliverable: A management exception query with conditional KPIs, aggregate labels, group filters, and stable priority sorting.
requirements: Use one source table and no joins or subqueries.
Use only concepts taught through Module 4.
Use thresholds that differ from lesson examples and Try It tasks.
Each progressive step must retain the complete prior solution.

modules.title: Final Capstone: Executive Sales Performance Report
modules.description: Plan, build, validate, and explain a complete single-table executive report using the full course toolkit.
modules.purpose: Integrate readable output, null-safe calculations, aggregate functions, grouping, HAVING, CASE, and conditional aggregation in one portfolio-quality report.
learningObjectives: Translate a reporting brief into metrics, grain, filters, aliases, and sorting rules.
Audit the source dataset before building final calculations.
Build a complete grouped executive report progressively.
Validate results and explain findings in plain business language.
guidedExercises: Map each stakeholder question to a SQL expression or clause.
Audit distinct categories, missing values, and date/status coverage.
Build the report from stable detail calculations into grouped KPIs.
Validate exact output and summarize two evidence-based findings.
modules.moduleProject: Complete the Executive Sales Performance Report as a progressive final capstone with a validated final query and written interpretation.

sections.title: Capstone Planning
sections.description: Convert stakeholder requirements into a precise report contract before writing the final query.

topics.title: Reading the Capstone Reporting Brief
topics.summary: Identify the audience, business questions, required metrics, filters, and output order.
learningGoals: Separate required output columns from supporting calculations.
Identify which requirements affect source rows and which affect groups.
State the expected report grain in one sentence.

topics.title: Building a Metric Map
topics.summary: Plan how each report column will be calculated and validated.
learningGoals: Map every requested KPI to the correct SQL function or expression.
Define aliases, rounding rules, and deterministic sorting.
Identify likely NULL and division risks before implementation.

sections.title: Capstone Data Readiness
sections.description: Audit the dataset and test the intended report grain before the final build.

topics.title: Auditing the sales_kpi Dataset
topics.summary: Run focused checks for available categories, statuses, ranges, and missing values.
learningGoals: Use DISTINCT and aggregates to inspect source-data coverage.
Count missing discount and rating values without changing the data.
Record the assumptions that the final report will use.

topics.title: Validating Report Grain and Order
topics.summary: Prototype grouping and sorting before adding every final KPI.
learningGoals: Confirm that each row represents one intended region-category group.
Use stable secondary sorting for tied values.
Detect an accidental grouping column before the final capstone build.

sections.title: Final Capstone Project
sections.description: Build the final executive report through cumulative, fully validated query snapshots.

topics.title: Final Capstone: Executive Sales Performance Report
topics.summary: Deliver a complete report that ranks region-category performance, calculates core and conditional KPIs, filters weak groups, and assigns a performance band.
learningGoals: Integrate every major course concept into one readable query.
Validate exact columns, aliases, values, labels, filters, and order.
Explain at least two findings using evidence from the final results table.

projectBrief.scenario: The leadership team needs one trustworthy report for its quarterly review. It must compare region-category performance, show sales and completion KPIs, exclude insignificant groups, and make priorities immediately visible.
projectBrief.role: Business intelligence analyst delivering to an executive audience
projectBrief.workspace: Browser SQL editor, schema browser, and deterministic results table
projectBrief.deliverable: A final single-table SQL report plus a concise plain-language interpretation of the validated results.
requirements: Use aliases, null-safe arithmetic, ROUND, aggregates, GROUP BY, WHERE, HAVING, CASE, and conditional aggregation.
Use no JOIN, subquery, CTE, window function, or mutation statement.
Build 8 progressive steps; each next starter query must equal the previous full solution query.
Use deterministic expected columns, rows, values, labels, and order at every graded step.
Use the shared capstone rubric and include a deliverable checklist.
The capstone scenario and thresholds must differ from all module projects and Try It tasks.
